question: Broad knowledge of human genetics & molecular biology
Broad knowledge of genetic and genomic analytical and testing methodologies
Deep knowledge of one or more programming languages to process and analyze ‘omics-level data
Critical evaluation of scientific and medical literature
Broad-based knowledge of the funding landscape for genomics research in Canada and internationally, including funding pools for commercialization, industry, and startup companies
Interpretation and use of major genetic and genomic databases (e.g., OMIM, gnomAD, ClinVar, COSMIC)
Reading, writing, and manipulating code in one or more programming languages (e.g., R, Python)
Distributed cluster / cloud computing, & working with big data (i.e., running pipelines using UNIX / command line)
Interpreting results / data in either a germline or somatic context
Application of ACMG and other professional guidelines in germline variant analysis (e.g., VCEP guidelines)
Application of ClinGen and other professional guidelines for gene curation
Assessment of case and functional studies for variant analysis
Assessment of in silico prediction for variant analysis
Following protocols, standard operating procedures (SOPs) and guidelines
Keeping a lab book & documenting lab work
Chairing / coordinating / hosting successful meetings
Seeking advice and asking for feedback / clarification from advisors and mentors when needed
Complying with rules, guidelines, policies, laws, and regulations
Adapting to changing goals, projects, and circumstances at work
Demonstrating accountability for one’s actions, and a dedication to acting in accordance with the highest standards of professionalism
Designing analysis pipelines for ‘omics data
Using code / pipelines written by others, with no (or with minor) modification, general maintenance of existing pipelines
Writing new code, or optimizing / heavily modifying existing code
Installing, testing, and debugging bioinformatics software for ‘omics data analysis
Performing research with pre-clinical samples (e.g., rodents, cell lines)
Performing literature searches and reviews
Critically reading scientific and medical literature
Using & interpreting databases of genetic and genomic information
Interpreting information on existing published variants
Interpreting information on novel variants
Implementing the ACMG and other important professional guidelines for germline variant analysis
Implementing ClinGen and other professional guidelines for gene curation
Providing actionable results that can impact patient care
Writing / editing scientific manuscripts
Writing / editing grant proposals
Researching, analyzing, and / or evaluating existing policy and programs related to genetic services
Writing position papers or policy papers
Maintaining a professional presence on social media (e.g., LinkedIn)
Representing data in figures / illustrations, creating visual communications (e.g., infographics, posters)
Communicating with / responding to a variety of stakeholders and cross-functional / multidisciplinary team members
Working directly with patients
Building relationships / working directly with clients and / or customers
Providing technical guidance on the application of products and policies related to genetic services
Recruiting and tracking patients in clinical trials, including patient consenting and data collection
Developing collaborations with industry (e.g., biotech / pharma)
Attending conferences or scientific meetings
Analyzing and managing financial data or budgets
Working on focused, specific projects
Planning or organizing events (e.g., conferences, tours, symposia, speaker series)
